PULSE — the dispatch loop
P1 - Always keep every worker slot busy by following this WORKFLOW in tandem with SWITCHBOARD.
P2 - Any input/trigger different than the ones expected > consult/follow SCRIPTS. Match is most-specific-wins; `*` is the fallback.

P3 - Re-run at tron:start AND on every ON-COMPLETE = pulse (slot free, *:clear, review done, recovered) AND when skill:session-end returns continue. (session:end may instead return `end`, which terminates the run — the selector lives in skill:session-end.) Then proceed to SWITCHBOARD.
SWITCHBOARD

Statuses: PENDING = todo (needs clearing) · CLEARED = ready to build · IN-PROGRESS = being built · BLOCKED = walled, awaiting operator · DONE · ABANDONED. Only CLEARED is dispatchable. Ignore IN-PROGRESS / DONE / BLOCKED / ABANDONED for selection. Cadence is PULL (checked here). On each pulse run, do all four steps in order:

1. FILL SLOTS — WHILE a worker slot is free AND dispatchable work exists, dispatch one block per free slot in priority: (a) oldest open CLEARED adhoc block (by ID); ELSE (b) a due cadence reviewer via cadence:<type> (advance/consume that counter); ELSE (c) the next CLEARED block by sequential ID via build:block:next. On dispatch, flip that block CLEARED>IN-PROGRESS so it is never re-picked by a later/concurrent run.

2. CLEAR AHEAD — for EVERY PENDING block not already in the architect's queue, enqueue the ARCHITECT via review:next:<block> (the queue has no slot limit; this keeps the CLEARED buffer ahead of the workers; architect-count sets drain speed).
3. WAIT — if no slot is free, or every remaining block is IN-PROGRESS / queued / BLOCKED, do nothing. The run re-enters on the next pulse (slot free, *:clear, operator:decision).

4. SESSION END — only if NO block is PENDING, CLEARED, IN-PROGRESS, or queued, no adhoc open, no cadence due, and all agents idle > trigger session:end. (BLOCKED blocks awaiting operator do NOT count as resolved — they hold the run open, not closed.)
NOTES

Fan-out: review:<type>:done fires BOTH Release reviewer AND Log Review — in parallel. (block:next:done fires only Wrap+release; clearing the next block is driven by SWITCHBOARD CLEAR AHEAD, not by a build-completion fan-out — this avoids double-enqueue.)

Cadence counter: each <type> counter increments on every block:next:done (one completed block). A <type> cadence is DUE when its count since the last <type> review >= its threshold; dispatch (SWITCHBOARD step 1b) resets it. Forward-review and adhoc-only work do not increment it.

Dispatch == spawn: build:block:next and cadence:<type> spawn the worker. The persistent architect is spawned once at Bootup. Liveness (heartbeat / stall / dead worker) is the ENGINE's job; worker:stalled is the only inbound trigger the engine feeds back to the table.

Architect is a PERSISTENT, dedicated agent (excluded from the worker slot pool) with a FIFO queue. Architect rows (Review block:next / Log Review) ENQUEUE work — they never require a free worker slot and never contend with engineers/reviewers. The architect drains its queue serially and emits *:clear. Architect throughput is the bottleneck; architect-count is the knob to raise it.

A wall parks its block as `blocked` and frees the slot; the block stays out of dispatch until the operator's operator:decision resumes/amends/abandons it. operator:decision is the inbound trigger for the operator's reply (escalate only emits the contact, not the reply).
Architect is always FORWARD-LOOKING: every review shapes upcoming blocks (clear next / adhoc). It never reopens a done block.

Branch selectors (the table names the alternatives; the selector lives where noted): done-vs-wall (Build & Review) = the worker's reported outcome; adhoc-vs-`-` (Log Review) = skill:log-review (findings or none); resume/amend/abandon (Apply operator decision) = the operator; slot-free-vs-wall (Recover) = skill:recover; end-vs-continue (Session End) = skill:session-end.
TRIGGER GRAMMAR
All triggers: lowercase, colon-delimited segments, no spaces.  Forms: domain:object (imperative dispatch) | subject:event (completion) | subject:object:event (qualified completion).

subjects/domains: tron | build | block | review | cadence | session | worker | operator | wall.  events/qualifiers: build | done | clear | stalled | next | end | adhoc | raised | decision.  <block> = a pipeline block ID (NN-NN-NN); block-specific triggers carry it: wall:raised:<block>, operator:decision:<block>, review:next:<block>, block:<block>:clear.
pulse is NOT a table-listener trigger — it is the dispatch control signal handled by PULSE P3 (re-run SWITCHBOARD). A linter should treat it as the one reserved control token, not match it to a row.

Matching: literal segments outrank <type>/<block> variables (most-specific-wins); `<type>` never binds the reserved literal `next`.  `*` = wildcard.  `|` = alternatives in one cell.  Terminals: `end` (session over) and `-` (no trigger).
